guideline.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary button.
guideline.rationale: Multiple primary buttons dilute visual hierarchy. When everything is emphasized, nothing is. A single primary button directs the user toward the most important action.

examples.title: Correct — single primary action
examples.description: A dialog footer with one primary Save button and one secondary Cancel button.

presentation.alt: A dialog footer with a secondary Cancel button on the left and a primary Save button on the right. Only the Save button has a filled blue background.

examples.title: Incorrect — competing primary actions
examples.description: Two primary buttons create competing focal points and dilute the visual hierarchy.

presentation.alt: A dialog footer with two primary buttons, both with filled blue backgrounds. Cancel and Save compete for visual attention.
